**Monmouthshire** ([Welsh](/wiki/Welsh_language "Welsh language"): *Sir Fynwy*) is a [constituency](/wiki/List_of_UK_Parliament_constituencies "List of UK Parliament constituencies") of the [House of Commons](/wiki/House_of_Commons_of_the_United_Kingdom "House of Commons of the United Kingdom") in the [UK Parliament](/wiki/UK_Parliament "UK Parliament"), and was first contested at the [2024 general election](/wiki/2024_United_Kingdom_general_election "2024 United Kingdom general election"), following the [2023 review of Westminster constituencies](/wiki/2023_review_of_Westminster_constituencies "2023 review of Westminster constituencies"). Its current MP is [Catherine Fookes](/wiki/Catherine_Fookes "Catherine Fookes"), a member of the [Labour Party](/wiki/Labour_Party_(UK) "Labour Party (UK)").

It previously existed as a [county constituency](/wiki/County_constituency "County constituency") of the [House of Commons](/wiki/House_of_Commons_of_the_United_Kingdom "House of Commons of the United Kingdom") of the [Parliament of England](/wiki/Parliament_of_England "Parliament of England") from 1536 until 1707, of the [Parliament of Great Britain](/wiki/Parliament_of_Great_Britain "Parliament of Great Britain") from 1707 to 1801, and of the [Parliament of the United Kingdom](/wiki/Parliament_of_the_United_Kingdom "Parliament of the United Kingdom") from 1801 to 1885. It elected two [Members of Parliament](/wiki/Member_of_Parliament_(United_Kingdom) "Member of Parliament (United Kingdom)") (MPs).

In 1885 the Monmouthshire constituency was divided to create [North Monmouthshire](/wiki/North_Monmouthshire "North Monmouthshire"), [South Monmouthshire](/wiki/South_Monmouthshire "South Monmouthshire") and [West Monmouthshire](/wiki/West_Monmouthshire "West Monmouthshire").

## History and boundaries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Monmouthshire_(UK_Parliament_constituency)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History and boundaries")]

Monmouthshire was given representation in the [Parliament of England](/wiki/Parliament_of_England "Parliament of England") by the [Laws in Wales Acts 1535 and 1542](/wiki/Laws_in_Wales_Acts_1535_and_1542 "Laws in Wales Acts 1535 and 1542"). This was later a key point in the long-standing argument about whether the county was or was not part of Wales.

Until the [Great Reform Act](/wiki/Reform_Act_1832 "Reform Act 1832") of 1832, the historic Monmouthshire [county constituency](/wiki/County_constituency "County constituency") covered almost the whole of the [county of Monmouth](/wiki/Monmouthshire_(historic) "Monmouthshire (historic)"), but not the county town of [Monmouth](/wiki/Monmouth "Monmouth"), which was a separate [borough constituency](/wiki/Borough_constituency "Borough constituency"). In 1832 this was expanded into the [Monmouth Boroughs](/wiki/Monmouth_Boroughs "Monmouth Boroughs"), taking in more towns to give it more electors.

The [Redistribution of Seats Act 1885](/wiki/Redistribution_of_Seats_Act_1885 "Redistribution of Seats Act 1885") split the county constituency into three, [North](/wiki/North_Monmouthshire_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "North Monmouthshire (UK Parliament constituency)"), [South](/wiki/South_Monmouthshire_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "South Monmouthshire (UK Parliament constituency)"), and [West Monmouthshire](/wiki/West_Monmouthshire_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "West Monmouthshire (UK Parliament constituency)"), and then the [Representation of the People Act 1918](/wiki/Representation_of_the_People_Act_1918 "Representation of the People Act 1918") abolished these, with most of the county joining a new seat called [Monmouth](/wiki/Monmouth_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Monmouth (UK Parliament constituency)"). This included the towns of [Chepstow](/wiki/Chepstow "Chepstow"), [Monmouth](/wiki/Monmouth "Monmouth"), and [Abergavenny](/wiki/Abergavenny "Abergavenny").

The Monmouthshire constituency was re-established as part of the 2023 review for the 2024 general election under the [June 2023 final proposals](/wiki/List_of_parliamentary_constituencies_in_Wales#Final_proposals "List of parliamentary constituencies in Wales") of the [Boundary Commission for Wales](/wiki/Boundary_Commission_for_Wales "Boundary Commission for Wales").[[2]](#cite_note-Final_proposals_report-2) The boundaries were defined as being coterminous with the [unitary authority](/wiki/Unitary_authority "Unitary authority") of the [County of Monmouthshire](/wiki/Monmouthshire "Monmouthshire"),[[3]](#cite_note-3)
so it does not now include those parts of the county in the area of the [Newport City Council](/wiki/Newport_City_Council "Newport City Council").
